questions.answerStructureHint: Discuss equivalent interaction, semantics, focus, instructions, feedback, touch, cognition, and testing.

expectedPoints.label: Provides an equivalent interaction
expectedPoints.description: Offers keyboard and non-drag controls that achieve the same task without relying on pointer precision or vision.

expectedPoints.label: Uses understandable semantics and feedback
expectedPoints.description: Gives controls accessible names, logical focus, concise instructions, announced state changes, and recoverable error feedback.

expectedPoints.label: Tests with complementary methods
expectedPoints.description: Combines semantic review and automated checks with keyboard, zoom, screen-reader, touch, and representative-user testing.

followUps.prompt: When is it better to replace a custom control with a native HTML element?

hu.title: Accessible student workflow review-ja
hu.prompt: Review-zz egy fiatal diákoknak készült custom drag-and-drop exercise-t. Milyen accessibility riskeket keresel, és hogyan terveznéd át?
hu.answerStructureHint: Beszélj equivalent interactionről, semanticsről, focusról, instructionről, feedbackről, touchról, cognitionről és testingről.

waf_equiv.label: Equivalent interactiont biztosít
waf_equiv.description: Keyboard és non-drag controlt ad ugyanahhoz a taskhoz pointer precision vagy látás szükségessége nélkül.

waf_semantics.label: Érthető semanticset és feedbacket használ
waf_semantics.description: Accessible nevet, logikus focust, rövid instrukciót, announced state change-et és recoverable error feedbacket ad.

waf_test.label: Kiegészítő módszerekkel tesztel
waf_test.description: Semantic review-t és automated checket keyboard, zoom, screen reader, touch és representative-user testinggel kombinál.

followUps.wingos_accessible_flow_001_followup: Mikor jobb egy custom controlt native HTML elemre cserélni?

questions.title: Building accessibility into delivery
questions.prompt: How would you make accessibility part of everyday feature delivery rather than a one-time audit before launch?
questions.answerStructureHint: Cover requirements, design, reusable components, automated and manual gates, user testing, ownership, and regression handling.

expectedPoints.label: Starts accessibility before implementation
expectedPoints.description: Adds accessibility acceptance criteria, keyboard flow, content clarity, and semantic expectations during discovery and design.

expectedPoints.label: Creates reusable safeguards
expectedPoints.description: Uses accessible design-system components, linting, component tests, automated scans, and documented patterns to prevent repeated mistakes.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps human verification and ownership
expectedPoints.description: Includes manual keyboard and assistive-technology checks, representative users where possible, named ownership, and regression triage.

followUps.prompt: Which accessibility checks would you block a pull request on?

hu.title: Accessibility beépítése a deliverybe
hu.prompt: Hogyan tennéd az accessibilityt a mindennapi feature delivery részévé egyszeri launch előtti audit helyett?
hu.answerStructureHint: Térj ki requirementre, designra, reusable componentre, automated és manual gate-re, user testingre, ownershipre és regression handlingre.

wap_early.label: Implementálás előtt kezdi az accessibilityt
wap_early.description: Accessibility acceptance criteriont, keyboard flow-t, content clarityt és semantic expectationt ad discovery és design közben.

wap_system.label: Reusable safeguardot készít
wap_system.description: Accessible design-system componentet, lintinget, component testet, automated scant és dokumentált patternt használ a visszatérő hibák ellen.

wap_manual.label: Megtartja a human verificationt és ownershipet
wap_manual.description: Manual keyboard és assistive-technology checket, lehetőség szerint representative usert, named ownershipet és regression triage-ot tartalmaz.

followUps.wingos_accessibility_process_001_followu: Mely accessibility check miatt blokkolnál egy pull requestet?

questions.title: Choosing Server and Client Components
questions.prompt: In a Next.js teacher dashboard, how do you decide what belongs in a Server Component and where to introduce a Client Component boundary?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain the default, the browser-only reasons for a client boundary, data and security implications, and bundle trade-offs.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from a server-first model
expectedPoints.description: Uses server rendering for trusted data access and non-interactive presentation, reducing client JavaScript and request waterfalls.

expectedPoints.label: Introduces a narrow client boundary
expectedPoints.description: Moves only code needing browser state, effects, events, or browser APIs into Client Components and passes serializable data across the boundary.

expectedPoints.label: Explains security and freshness trade-offs
expectedPoints.description: Keeps secrets and authorization on the server, and chooses cache or revalidation rules based on whether data is public, shared, or personalized.

followUps.prompt: What symptoms suggest that the client boundary is too high in the component tree?

hu.title: Server és Client Component választása
hu.prompt: Egy Next.js teacher dashboardban hogyan döntöd el, mi kerüljön Server Componentbe, és hol legyen Client Component boundary?
hu.answerStructureHint: Magyarázd el a defaultot, a client boundary browser-only okait, a data- és security implicationt, valamint a bundle trade-offot.

wnb_default.label: Server-first modellből indul
wnb_default.description: Server renderinget használ trusted data accesshez és non-interactive megjelenítéshez, csökkentve a client JavaScriptet és request waterfallt.

wnb_client.label: Keskeny client boundary-t vezet be
wnb_client.description: Csak browser state-et, effectet, eventet vagy browser API-t igénylő kódot tesz Client Componentbe, serializable adatot adva át.

wnb_tradeoff.label: Elmagyarázza a security és freshness trade-offot
wnb_tradeoff.description: Secretet és authorizationt szerveren tart, cache- vagy revalidation szabályt pedig public, shared vagy personalized adat alapján választ.

followUps.wingos_next_boundary_001_followup: Milyen tünet jelzi, hogy a client boundary túl magasan van a component tree-ben?

questions.title: Avoiding stale or leaked data in Next.js
questions.prompt: A teacher sees another user’s cached dashboard data after a deployment. How would you investigate and redesign the data-fetching path?
questions.answerStructureHint: Trace cache scope and keys, separate public from personalized data, enforce server authorization, and add regression protection.

expectedPoints.label: Contains the privacy incident
expectedPoints.description: Disables the affected cache or route, assesses exposure, preserves evidence, and follows the incident and notification process.

expectedPoints.label: Finds the cache-scope error

expectedPoints.label: Designs a safe personalized path

followUps.prompt: When is caching personalized data still reasonable?

hu.title: Stale vagy leaked adat elkerülése Next.js-ben

hu.answerStructureHint: Trace-eld a cache scope-ot és keyt, válaszd szét a public és personalized adatot, kényszeríts server authorizationt és adj regression protectiont.

wnc_contain.label: Containeli a privacy incidentet
wnc_contain.description: Kikapcsolja az érintett cache-t vagy route-ot, felméri az exposure-t, megőrzi az evidence-et és követi az incident- és notification processt.

wnc_root.label: Megtalálja a cache-scope hibát
wnc_root.description: Ellenőrzi a static renderinget, shared fetch cache-t, route cache-t, CDN-t, cache keyt, cookie-t, headert és hogy authorization történt-e a cache selection előtt.

wnc_fix.label: Biztonságos personalized pathot tervez
wnc_fix.description: User adatot szükség szerint dynamic vagy no-store állapotba tesz, safe cache-t authorization contexttel keyel, és cross-user isolation testet, valamint monitoringot ad.

followUps.wingos_next_cache_001_followup: Mikor lehet mégis indokolt personalized adatot cache-elni?

questions.title: Structuring a Node.js service
questions.prompt: How would you structure a Node.js API so that business rules can be tested without Express, a real database, or an LLM provider?
questions.answerStructureHint: Describe responsibilities at the transport, application/domain, and infrastructure boundaries, then show how dependencies and errors move between them.

expectedPoints.label: Separates responsibilities
expectedPoints.description: Keeps HTTP parsing and response mapping in controllers, business decisions in use cases, and database or provider details in adapters.

expectedPoints.label: Uses explicit dependency direction
expectedPoints.description: Makes core logic depend on narrow interfaces and injects concrete infrastructure from the application composition root.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps errors meaningful across layers
expectedPoints.description: Uses domain or application errors internally, maps them once to stable HTTP responses, and records unexpected failures with correlation context.

followUps.prompt: When does this separation become unnecessary ceremony?

hu.title: Node.js service strukturálása
hu.prompt: Hogyan strukturálnál egy Node.js API-t úgy, hogy a business rule Express, valódi database vagy LLM provider nélkül is tesztelhető legyen?
hu.answerStructureHint: Írd le a transport, application/domain és infrastructure boundary felelősségeit, majd mutasd meg a dependency és error áramlását.

wnl_boundaries.label: Szétválasztja a felelősségeket
wnl_boundaries.description: Az HTTP parsingot és response mappinget controllerben, a business decisiont use case-ben, a database- vagy provider-részletet adapterben tartja.

wnl_dependencies.label: Explicit dependency directiont használ
wnl_dependencies.description: A core logic keskeny interface-től függ, a concrete infrastructure pedig application composition rootból kerül injectálásra.

wnl_errors.label: Jelentést tart meg layer-ek között az erroroknak
wnl_errors.description: Belsőleg domain vagy application errort használ, egyszer mapeli stabil HTTP response-ra, az unexpected failure-t pedig correlation contexttel rögzíti.

followUps.wingos_node_layers_001_followup: Mikor válik ez a separation felesleges ceremonyvá?

questions.title: Protecting the Node.js event loop
questions.prompt: An endpoint that parses uploaded learning material makes every other request slow. Explain the likely cause and the changes you would consider.
questions.answerStructureHint: Distinguish blocking CPU work from asynchronous I/O, measure the event loop, and choose bounded offloading or streaming with operational safeguards.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies event-loop blocking
expectedPoints.description: Explains that synchronous parsing or CPU-heavy transformation blocks the main JavaScript thread and proposes profiling event-loop delay and CPU.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses an appropriate execution model
expectedPoints.description: Considers streaming, worker threads, a separate worker service, or a queue based on work size, latency need, isolation, and deployment complexity.

expectedPoints.label: Adds overload protection
expectedPoints.description: Uses file limits, bounded concurrency, timeouts, cancellation, backpressure, progress state, and safe retry or idempotency where needed.

followUps.prompt: Would worker threads or a separate service be your default, and what changes that choice?

hu.title: A Node.js event loop védelme
hu.prompt: Egy feltöltött learning materialt feldolgozó endpoint minden más requestet lelassít. Magyarázd el a valószínű okot és a lehetséges változtatásokat.
hu.answerStructureHint: Válaszd szét a blocking CPU worköt az asynchronous I/O-tól, mérd az event loopot, és válassz bounded offloadingot vagy streaminget operational safeguarddal.

wne_diagnose.label: Azonosítja az event-loop blockingot
wne_diagnose.description: Elmagyarázza, hogy a synchronous parsing vagy CPU-heavy transformation blokkolja a fő JavaScript szálat, és event-loop delay, valamint CPU profilingot javasol.

wne_offload.label: Megfelelő execution modelt választ
wne_offload.description: Streaminget, worker threadet, külön worker service-t vagy queue-t mérlegel work size, latency need, isolation és deployment complexity alapján.

wne_limits.label: Overload protectiont ad
wne_limits.description: File limitet, bounded concurrencyt, timeoutot, cancellationt, backpressure-t, progress state-et és szükség szerint safe retryt vagy idempotencyt használ.

followUps.wingos_node_event_loop_001_followup: Worker thread vagy külön service lenne a default, és mi változtatná meg a döntést?

questions.title: Managing resources in FastAPI
questions.prompt: How would you manage database pools and LLM clients in FastAPI without creating them per request or leaking them at shutdown?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain lifespan, dependency scopes, per-request sessions, shared clients, cleanup, and async compatibility.

expectedPoints.label: Uses application lifespan for shared resources
expectedPoints.description: Creates long-lived pools or provider clients during startup, stores them in controlled application state, and closes them during shutdown.

expectedPoints.label: Scopes request resources correctly
expectedPoints.description: Provides a short-lived database session or transaction through a dependency and guarantees commit, rollback, and cleanup.

expectedPoints.label: Checks async and concurrency behaviour
expectedPoints.description: Avoids blocking clients in async endpoints or isolates them, configures pool and timeout limits, and tests graceful shutdown under active requests.

followUps.prompt: What problems appear when a database session is stored in a global variable?

hu.title: Resource-kezelés FastAPI-ban
hu.prompt: Hogyan kezelnéd a database poolt és LLM clientet FastAPI-ban anélkül, hogy requestenként létrehoznád vagy shutdownkor leakelnéd?
hu.answerStructureHint: Magyarázd el a lifespan, dependency scope, per-request session, shared client, cleanup és async compatibility kérdéseit.

wfl_lifespan.label: Application lifespant használ shared resource-hoz
wfl_lifespan.description: Startupkor hoz létre long-lived poolt vagy provider clientet, controlled application state-ben tárolja és shutdownkor lezárja.

wfl_request.label: Helyesen scope-olja a request resource-ot
wfl_request.description: Dependencyn keresztül short-lived database sessiont vagy transactiont ad, és garantálja a commitot, rollbacket, valamint cleanupot.

wfl_async.label: Ellenőrzi az async és concurrency viselkedést
wfl_async.description: Kerüli vagy izolálja a blocking clientet async endpointban, pool- és timeout limitet konfigurál, és active request mellett teszteli a graceful shutdownt.

followUps.wingos_fastapi_lifecycle_001_followup: Milyen problémát okoz, ha a database session global variable-ben van?

questions.title: Choosing Node.js or FastAPI for an AI service
questions.prompt: A TypeScript product needs a new AI-assisted backend capability. How would you decide whether to add it to Node.js or create a Python FastAPI service?
questions.answerStructureHint: Compare capability fit, team ownership, libraries, deployment, data boundaries, scaling, failure modes, and the cost of another service.

expectedPoints.label: Starts with the simplest ownership model
expectedPoints.description: Prefers extending the existing service when requirements and libraries fit, avoiding a network boundary created only for language preference.

expectedPoints.label: Names legitimate reasons for Python separation
expectedPoints.description: Considers Python-specific model libraries, independent scaling, heavy compute isolation, specialist ownership, or a clear bounded capability.

expectedPoints.label: Accounts for distributed-system cost
expectedPoints.description: Includes API contracts, authentication, observability, deployment, retries, versioning, data consistency, and on-call responsibility in the decision.

followUps.prompt: What evidence would justify extracting the capability later?

hu.title: Node.js vagy FastAPI választása AI service-hez
hu.prompt: Egy TypeScript productnak új AI-assisted backend capability kell. Hogyan döntenéd el, hogy Node.js-be kerüljön vagy külön Python FastAPI service készüljön?
hu.answerStructureHint: Hasonlítsd össze a capability fitet, team ownershipet, libraryket, deploymentet, data boundaryt, scalinget, failure mode-ot és egy új service költségét.

wnf_default.label: A legegyszerűbb ownership modelből indul
wnf_default.description: Az existing service bővítését preferálja, ha a requirement és library illik, és nem hoz létre network boundary-t pusztán language preference miatt.

wnf_reasons.label: Valós okokat nevez meg Python separationre
wnf_reasons.description: Python-specifikus model libraryt, independent scalinget, heavy compute isolationt, specialist ownershipet vagy világos bounded capabilityt mérlegel.

wnf_cost.label: Számol a distributed-system costtal
wnf_cost.description: A döntésbe beveszi az API contractot, authenticationt, observabilityt, deploymentet, retryt, versioninget, data consistencyt és on-call felelősséget.

followUps.wingos_node_vs_fastapi_001_followup: Milyen evidence indokolná a capability későbbi extractálását?

questions.title: Designing useful API errors
questions.prompt: Design the error contract for an endpoint that creates an assignment. How should validation, authorization, conflict, dependency, and unexpected failures differ?
questions.answerStructureHint: Cover status semantics, stable codes, safe details, field errors, traceability, retry guidance, and internal logging.

expectedPoints.label: Uses meaningful failure categories
expectedPoints.description: Maps invalid input, unauthenticated or forbidden access, version conflict, unavailable dependency, and unknown bugs to distinct status and code semantics.

expectedPoints.label: Returns a stable safe error shape
expectedPoints.description: Includes a machine-readable code, user-safe message, optional field details, request or correlation id, and retryability without exposing stack traces.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves diagnostic context internally
expectedPoints.description: Logs structured cause chains and relevant non-sensitive context, records dependency details internally, and alerts according to user impact.

followUps.prompt: Should the API reveal that a requested classroom exists when the caller is unauthorized?

hu.title: Hasznos API error tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezd meg egy assignmentet létrehozó endpoint error contractját. Hogyan különbözzön a validation, authorization, conflict, dependency és unexpected failure?
hu.answerStructureHint: Térj ki status semanticsre, stable code-ra, safe detailre, field errorra, traceabilityre, retry guidance-ra és internal loggingra.

wae_semantics.label: Értelmes failure categoryt használ
wae_semantics.description: Az invalid inputot, unauthenticated vagy forbidden accesst, version conflictot, unavailable dependencyt és unknown bugot külön status- és code semanticre mapeli.

wae_contract.label: Stabil és safe error shape-et ad
wae_contract.description: Machine-readable code-ot, user-safe message-et, optional field detailt, request vagy correlation id-t és retryabilityt ad stack trace nélkül.

wae_internal.label: Belső diagnostic contextet megőriz
wae_internal.description: Structured cause chain-t és releváns non-sensitive contextet logol, provider-részletet belül rögzít, és user impact alapján alertel.

followUps.wingos_api_errors_001_followup: Felfedje-e az API, hogy a kért classroom létezik, ha a caller unauthorized?

questions.title: Runtime validation in TypeScript and Python
questions.prompt: Why are TypeScript types or Python annotations not enough at an API boundary, and where would you validate data in a full-stack system?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain erased or non-enforcing types, trust boundaries, shared schemas, business validation, output validation, and duplicated UX checks.

expectedPoints.label: Explains the runtime gap
expectedPoints.description: States that external JSON is untrusted, TypeScript types are erased, and annotations alone do not prove that a received value matches the declared shape.

expectedPoints.label: Places validation at the right layers
expectedPoints.description: Uses client checks for fast UX, server schema validation for trust, domain validation for business rules, and response validation at risky external-provider boundaries.

expectedPoints.label: Controls schema reuse and drift
expectedPoints.description: Considers generated clients or shared schema definitions while keeping server enforcement authoritative and versioning contract changes.

followUps.prompt: What validation belongs in the database even after API validation succeeds?

hu.title: Runtime validation TypeScriptben és Pythonban
hu.prompt: Miért nem elég a TypeScript type vagy Python annotation egy API boundary-n, és hol validálnád az adatot egy full-stack rendszerben?
hu.answerStructureHint: Magyarázd el az erased vagy non-enforcing type-ot, trust boundary-t, shared schemát, business validationt, output validationt és duplicated UX checket.

wrv_runtime.label: Elmagyarázza a runtime gapet
wrv_runtime.description: Kimondja, hogy az external JSON untrusted, a TypeScript type erased, és az annotation önmagában nem bizonyítja, hogy a received value megfelel a declared shape-nek.

wrv_layers.label: Jó layer-ekre helyezi a validationt
wrv_layers.description: Client checket használ gyors UX-re, server schema validationt trusthoz, domain validationt business rule-hoz és response validationt kockázatos external-provider boundary-n.

wrv_schema.label: Kontrollálja a schema reuse-t és driftet
wrv_schema.description: Generated clientet vagy shared schema definitiont mérlegel, de a server enforcement marad authoritative, a contract change pedig versioned.

followUps.wingos_runtime_validation_001_followup: Milyen validation tartozik a database-be akkor is, ha az API validation sikeres?

questions.title: Designing the classroom data model
questions.prompt: Sketch a PostgreSQL model for schools, teachers, classes, students, assignments, content versions, and submissions. Explain the most important constraints and indexes.
questions.answerStructureHint: State ownership and cardinality, then walk through invariants, versioning, authorization queries, transactions, and access-pattern-driven indexes.

expectedPoints.label: Models entities and relationships clearly
expectedPoints.description: Uses membership or join tables for many-to-many relations, separates reusable content from assigned instances, and preserves submitted content versions.

expectedPoints.label: Enforces business invariants
expectedPoints.description: Uses foreign keys, unique constraints, checks, and transactions to prevent duplicate membership, invalid ownership, orphan records, and conflicting submission state.

expectedPoints.label: Designs indexes from queries
expectedPoints.description: Names common teacher and student access patterns, chooses composite index order accordingly, and notes the write and storage cost of unnecessary indexes.

followUps.prompt: How would you support editing an assignment without changing what a student previously submitted against?

hu.title: Classroom data model tervezése
hu.prompt: Vázolj PostgreSQL modellt school, teacher, class, student, assignment, content version és submission számára. Magyarázd el a legfontosabb constraintet és indexet.
hu.answerStructureHint: Mondd el az ownershipet és cardinalityt, majd menj végig az invarianton, versioningen, authorization queryn, transactionön és access-pattern-driven indexen.

wsc_model.label: Világosan modellezi az entityket és kapcsolatokat
wsc_model.description: Membership vagy join tablet használ many-to-many relationhöz, szétválasztja a reusable contentet az assigned instance-tól és megőrzi a submitted content versiont.

wsc_constraints.label: Kikényszeríti a business invariantot
wsc_constraints.description: Foreign keyt, unique constraintet, checket és transactiont használ duplicate membership, invalid ownership, orphan record és conflicting submission state ellen.

wsc_indexes.label: Queryből tervezi az indexet
wsc_indexes.description: Megnevezi a gyakori teacher és student access patternt, ennek megfelelő composite index ordert választ, és megemlíti a felesleges index write- és storage costját.

questions.title: Handling concurrent submissions
questions.prompt: Two browser tabs submit updates to the same student answer almost simultaneously. How would you prevent silent data loss?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain the consistency requirement, compare optimistic and pessimistic approaches, define conflict response and UX, and cover retries or idempotency.

expectedPoints.label: Detects stale writes
expectedPoints.description: Uses a version number, updated-at token, or ETag in the update condition so a stale client cannot overwrite a newer row silently.

expectedPoints.label: Handles conflict explicitly
expectedPoints.description: Returns a conflict result with current safe state and lets the user reload, compare, or merge instead of silently choosing the last write.

expectedPoints.label: Matches locking to the workflow
expectedPoints.description: Explains when short transactions or row locks may be justified, and separates duplicate request handling through idempotency from concurrent editing conflicts.

followUps.prompt: Would autosave change your approach?

hu.title: Concurrent submission kezelése
hu.prompt: Két browser tab szinte egyszerre küld update-et ugyanahhoz a student answerhez. Hogyan akadályoznád meg a silent data losst?
hu.answerStructureHint: Magyarázd el a consistency requirementet, hasonlítsd össze az optimistic és pessimistic megoldást, definiáld a conflict response-t és UX-et, majd térj ki retryra vagy idempotencyre.

wcs_detect.label: Észleli a stale write-ot
wcs_detect.description: Version numbert, updated-at tokent vagy ETaget használ az update conditionben, így stale client nem írhatja felül csendben az újabb sort.

wcs_conflict.label: Explicit kezeli a conflictot
wcs_conflict.description: Conflict resultot ad az aktuális safe state-tel, és reloadot, compare-t vagy merge-et kínál silent last-write-wins helyett.

wcs_tradeoff.label: A workflow-hoz illő lockingot választ
wcs_tradeoff.description: Elmagyarázza, mikor indokolt short transaction vagy row lock, és szétválasztja a duplicate request idempotency-kezelését a concurrent edit conflicttól.

questions.title: Designing an LLM-assisted feature
questions.prompt: Design a feature that generates draft exercises from teacher-provided source material. The teacher must review the result before students see it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Cover the user workflow, trusted boundary, context and prompt construction, structured output, validation, review, persistence, privacy, evaluation, and failure handling.

expectedPoints.label: Places the model behind a controlled boundary
expectedPoints.description: Keeps provider calls server-side, minimizes and prepares context, uses a provider adapter, and prevents model output from directly changing trusted state.

expectedPoints.label: Validates and reviews generated content
expectedPoints.description: Requests constrained structured output, validates schema and product rules, applies content-safety checks, and stores a draft requiring explicit teacher approval.

expectedPoints.label: Designs for operation and learning
expectedPoints.description: Includes model and prompt version metadata, privacy-safe telemetry, evaluation gates, timeout and retry policy, fallback UX, cost controls, and auditability.

followUps.prompt: What part of the workflow must remain deterministic even if the model improves?

hu.title: LLM-assisted feature tervezése

wla_boundary.label: Kontrollált boundary mögé teszi a modellt
wla_boundary.description: Server-side tartja a provider callt, minimalizálja és előkészíti a contextet, provider adaptert használ, és nem engedi, hogy a model output közvetlen trusted state-et módosítson.

wla_validate.label: Validálja és review-zza a generated contentet
wla_validate.description: Constrained structured outputot kér, schema- és product rule-t validál, content-safety checket alkalmaz, és explicit teacher approvalt igénylő draftot tárol.

wla_operate.label: Operationre és tanulásra tervez
wla_operate.description: Model- és prompt-version metadata-t, privacy-safe telemetryt, evaluation gate-et, timeout- és retry policyt, fallback UX-et, cost controlt és auditabilityt tartalmaz.

followUps.wingos_llm_architecture_001_followup: A workflow mely része maradjon deterministic akkor is, ha a modell javul?

questions.title: Synchronous or asynchronous AI generation

questions.answerStructureHint: Compare interaction needs and complexity, then describe job state, idempotency, cancellation, progress delivery, retries, and cleanup.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses from the user experience and latency
expectedPoints.description: Keeps short interactive work synchronous but moves long, optional, or variable generation to a durable job so normal navigation and retries remain safe.

expectedPoints.label: Models job lifecycle explicitly
expectedPoints.description: Defines queued, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled, and expired states with ownership, progress or heartbeat, result reference, and idempotent creation.

expectedPoints.label: Handles results and failures safely
expectedPoints.description: Uses polling, SSE, or WebSocket according to need, applies bounded retries and dead-letter handling, and cleans up abandoned jobs and sensitive temporary data.

followUps.prompt: When would a long synchronous request still be the better choice?

hu.title: Synchronous vagy asynchronous AI generation
hu.prompt: Egy LLM request gyakran 15–40 másodpercig tart. Nyitva tartanád a browser requestet, vagy background jobba vinnéd a munkát?
hu.answerStructureHint: Hasonlítsd össze az interaction needet és complexityt, majd írd le a job state-et, idempotencyt, cancellationt, progress deliveryt, retryt és cleanupot.

wlas_choice.label: A UX és latency alapján választ
wlas_choice.description: A rövid interactive munkát synchronous állapotban tartja, a hosszú, optional vagy változó generationt durable jobba viszi, hogy a navigation és retry safe maradjon.

wlas_state.label: Explicit job lifecycle-t modellez
wlas_state.description: Queued, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled és expired state-et definiál ownershippel, progress- vagy heartbeat-jellel, result reference-szel és idempotent creationnel.

wlas_delivery.label: Biztonságosan kezeli az eredményt és failure-t
wlas_delivery.description: Pollingot, SSE-t vagy WebSocketet használ az igény szerint, bounded retryt és dead-letter handlinget alkalmaz, és takarítja az abandoned jobot, valamint sensitive temporary datát.

followUps.wingos_llm_async_001_followup: Mikor lehet mégis jobb egy hosszú synchronous request?

questions.title: Designing authorized multilingual RAG

expectedPoints.label: Builds a traceable content index

expectedPoints.label: Filters by authorization before relevance

expectedPoints.description: Combines language-aware retrieval or reranking with limited context, asks for source-bound answers, displays references, handles no-evidence cases, and evaluates retrieval separately from generation.

hu.title: Authorized multilingual RAG tervezése

wle_gate.label: Segmentált release gate-et használ
wle_gate.description: Baseline-nal hasonlít össze language és task type szerint, latencyt és costot is mér, unacceptable failure-t definiál, és nem csak average score-t kér.

questions.title: Investigating an LLM quality regression
questions.prompt: Teacher ratings dropped after a release that changed the prompt, retrieval settings, and provider model. How would you isolate the cause?
questions.answerStructureHint: Contain the impact, compare versioned evidence, segment the regression, isolate one variable at a time, and improve release discipline.

expectedPoints.label: Limits user impact
expectedPoints.description: Pauses exposure, rolls back or switches the model or prompt through versioned configuration, and preserves affected requests and metrics within privacy limits.

expectedPoints.label: Finds where quality changed
expectedPoints.description: Compares evaluation and production evidence by language, task, source type, failure category, prompt version, retrieval result, and model version.

expectedPoints.label: Uses controlled comparisons
expectedPoints.description: Replays a privacy-safe case set with one variable changed at a time or a small factorial experiment, then adds a regression case and safer release gate.

followUps.prompt: What if offline evaluation improves but teacher ratings still fall?

hu.title: LLM quality regression kivizsgálása
hu.prompt: Teacher rating romlott egy olyan release után, amely promptot, retrieval settinget és provider modellt is változtatott. Hogyan izolálnád az okot?
hu.answerStructureHint: Containeld a hatást, hasonlíts össze versioned evidence-et, segmentáld a regressiont, izolálj egyszerre egy változót, és javítsd a release discipline-t.

wlr_contain.label: Korlátozza a user impactot
wlr_contain.description: Megállítja az exposure-t, rollbackel vagy versioned configurationnel modellt vagy promptot vált, és privacy limiteken belül megőrzi az érintett requestet és metricet.

wlr_segment.label: Megtalálja, hol változott a quality
wlr_segment.description: Evaluation- és production evidence-et hasonlít össze language, task, source type, failure category, prompt version, retrieval result és model version szerint.

wlr_isolate.label: Controlled comparisont használ
wlr_isolate.description: Privacy-safe case setet replayel egyszerre egy változóval vagy kis factorial experimenttel, majd regression case-et és biztonságosabb release gate-et ad.

followUps.wingos_llm_regression_001_followup: Mi van akkor, ha az offline evaluation javul, de a teacher rating mégis esik?

questions.title: Defending an LLM tool from prompt injection
questions.prompt: An assistant reads uploaded documents and can create draft assignments. A document tells the model to ignore its rules and publish private teacher notes. How should the system respond?
questions.answerStructureHint: Treat content as untrusted data, enforce capability and authorization outside the model, minimize context, validate the action, and preserve human approval.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps a strict trust boundary
expectedPoints.description: Treats uploaded text as data, not policy, separates instructions from retrieved content, and never places secrets or unrelated privileged records in the prompt.

expectedPoints.label: Enforces permissions outside the model
expectedPoints.description: Uses trusted user identity, least-privilege tool credentials, fixed capability schemas, and server-side authorization for every read and write.

expectedPoints.label: Validates and contains model actions
expectedPoints.description: Allows only draft creation, validates arguments and content policy, requires teacher confirmation for publication, logs the attempted action safely, and tests adversarial cases.

followUps.prompt: Why is a stronger system prompt not a complete defence?

hu.title: LLM tool védelme prompt injection ellen
hu.prompt: Egy assistant feltöltött dokumentumokat olvas és draft assignmentet készíthet. Egy dokumentum arra utasítja a modellt, hogy hagyja figyelmen kívül a szabályait és publisholjon private teacher note-ot. Hogyan reagáljon a rendszer?
hu.answerStructureHint: Kezeld a contentet untrusted dataként, a modellen kívül kényszerítsd ki a capabilityt és authorizationt, minimalizáld a contextet, validáld az actiont és tartsd meg a human approvalt.

wpi_trust.label: Szigorú trust boundary-t tart
wpi_trust.description: A feltöltött textet adatként, nem policyként kezeli, elválasztja az instructiont a retrieved contenttől, és secretet vagy unrelated privileged recordot nem tesz promptba.

wpi_auth.label: A modellen kívül kényszeríti ki a permissiont
wpi_auth.description: Trusted user identityt, least-privilege tool credentialt, fixed capability schemát és minden read/write actionhöz server-side authorizationt használ.

wpi_action.label: Validálja és containeli a model actiont
wpi_action.description: Csak draft creationt enged, validálja az argumentumot és content policyt, publicationhöz teacher confirmationt kér, safe módon logolja az attempted actiont és adversarial case-t tesztel.

followUps.wingos_prompt_injection_001_followup: Miért nem teljes védelem egy erősebb system prompt?

questions.title: Privacy review of an AI feature
questions.prompt: Before sending student work to an external LLM provider, what technical questions and controls would you require?
questions.answerStructureHint: Map necessity and data flow, minimize or pseudonymize, assess provider and region, control retention and access, secure logging, and support deletion and auditing.

expectedPoints.label: Challenges data necessity
expectedPoints.description: Defines the exact feature purpose and sends only required text or attributes, excluding full profiles, unrelated history, direct identifiers, and secrets.

expectedPoints.label: Assesses provider processing
expectedPoints.description: Checks storage and training use, retention, subprocessors, region and transfer, encryption, access controls, incident terms, deletion, and available enterprise settings.

expectedPoints.label: Controls internal data lifecycle
expectedPoints.description: Restricts access, redacts logs, separates audit metadata from raw prompts, defines retention and deletion propagation, and monitors unusual provider usage.

followUps.prompt: How would you debug a bad model response if raw prompts are not kept?

hu.title: AI feature privacy review-ja
hu.prompt: Mielőtt student worköt küldesz external LLM providernek, milyen technikai kérdést és kontrollt követelnél meg?
hu.answerStructureHint: Térképezd fel a necessityt és data flow-t, minimalizálj vagy pseudonymizálj, értékeld a providert és regiont, kontrolláld a retentiont és accesst, secure-olj loggingot, és támogasd a deletiont, valamint auditingot.

wapr_need.label: Megkérdőjelezi az adat szükségességét
wapr_need.description: Definiálja a feature pontos purpose-ét, és csak szükséges textet vagy attribútumot küld, kizárva a full profile-t, unrelated historyt, direct identifiert és secretet.

wapr_provider.label: Értékeli a provider processinget
wapr_provider.description: Ellenőrzi a storage- és training use-t, retentiont, subprocessort, regiont és transfert, encryptiont, access controlt, incident termet, deletiont és enterprise settinget.

wapr_ops.label: Kontrollálja a belső data lifecycle-t
wapr_ops.description: Korlátozza az accesst, redactálja a logot, szétválasztja az audit metadata-t a raw prompttól, retentiont és deletion propagationt definiál, és unusual provider usage-ot monitoroz.

followUps.wingos_ai_privacy_001_followup: Hogyan debugolnál rossz model response-t, ha a raw promptot nem tároljátok?

questions.title: Designing for an LLM provider outage
questions.prompt: The LLM provider is unavailable during a school day. How should the product degrade, recover, and communicate the problem?
questions.answerStructureHint: Separate essential and optional paths, fail quickly after bounded attempts, preserve work, communicate status, recover safely, and learn from the incident.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves the core workflow
expectedPoints.description: Keeps manual creation, saved drafts, viewing, and other essential tasks available while clearly marking AI assistance as temporarily unavailable.

expectedPoints.label: Prevents a retry storm
expectedPoints.description: Uses short timeouts, error classification, limited exponential backoff with jitter, circuit breaking, bounded queues, and provider or feature-level rate controls.

expectedPoints.label: Recovers and verifies safely
expectedPoints.description: Resumes queued work deliberately, protects idempotency, communicates delayed or failed jobs, verifies quality after provider recovery, and reviews SLO and incident evidence.

followUps.prompt: Would you automatically fail over to another model provider?

hu.title: LLM provider outage kezelése
hu.prompt: Az LLM provider unavailable egy tanítási napon. Hogyan degrade-eljen, recovereljen és kommunikálja a problémát a product?
hu.answerStructureHint: Válaszd szét az essential és optional pathot, bounded attempt után fail quickly, őrizd meg a munkát, kommunikáld a statust, safe módon recoverelj és tanulj az incidentből.

wlo_degrade.label: Megőrzi a core workflow-t
wlo_degrade.description: A manual creationt, saved draftot, viewingot és más essential taskot elérhetően tart, az AI assistance-t pedig világosan temporarily unavailable állapotúnak jelzi.

wlo_protect.label: Megakadályozza a retry stormot
wlo_protect.description: Rövid timeoutot, error classificationt, limitált exponential backoffot jitterrel, circuit breakert, bounded queue-t és provider- vagy feature-level rate controlt használ.

wlo_recover.label: Safe módon recoverel és verify-ol
wlo_recover.description: Tudatosan folytatja a queued worköt, védi az idempotencyt, kommunikálja a delayed vagy failed jobot, provider recovery után qualityt ellenőriz, és review-zza az SLO- és incident evidence-et.

questions.title: Controlling LLM cost without harming value
questions.prompt: LLM spending doubles after adoption grows. How would you reduce cost without blindly lowering output quality?
questions.answerStructureHint: Attribute spend to features and outcomes, remove waste, then evaluate model, context, caching, batching, quota, and product trade-offs against quality.

expectedPoints.label: Attributes cost to product behaviour
expectedPoints.description: Measures requests, input and output tokens, retries, latency, cache hits, model, language, user segment, feature, and accepted or edited outcomes.

expectedPoints.label: Removes avoidable work first
expectedPoints.description: Finds duplicate calls, runaway retries, oversized context, unused output, accidental test traffic, and requests that could be deterministic or deferred.

expectedPoints.label: Optimizes under a quality guardrail
expectedPoints.description: Tests smaller models, prompt and retrieval changes, bounded caching, batching, quotas, and tiered service against segmented evaluation and user-value metrics.

followUps.prompt: Which generated results are safe to cache, and what belongs in the cache key?

hu.title: LLM cost kontroll value-romlás nélkül
hu.prompt: Az LLM spending megduplázódik az adoption növekedésével. Hogyan csökkentenéd a costot anélkül, hogy vakon rontanád az output qualityt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Rendeld a spendet feature-höz és outcome-hoz, szüntesd meg a waste-et, majd quality ellenében értékeld a model, context, caching, batching, quota és product trade-offot.

wlc_measure.label: Product behaviourhöz rendeli a costot
wlc_measure.description: Requestet, input- és output tokent, retryt, latencyt, cache hitet, modellt, language-et, user segmentet, feature-t és accepted vagy edited outcome-ot mér.

wlc_waste.label: Először az elkerülhető munkát szünteti meg
wlc_waste.description: Megkeresi a duplicate callt, runaway retryt, oversized contextet, unused outputot, accidental test trafficet és deterministic vagy deferred módon megoldható requestet.

wlc_tradeoff.label: Quality guardrail mellett optimalizál
wlc_tradeoff.description: Kisebb modellt, prompt- és retrieval change-et, bounded cachinget, batchinget, quotát és tiered service-t tesztel segmented evaluation és user-value metric ellen.

followUps.wingos_llm_cost_001_followup: Mely generated result cache-elhető biztonságosan, és mi tartozik a cache keybe?

questions.title: Designing the delivery pipeline
questions.prompt: Design a CI/CD pipeline for a Next.js frontend, Node.js or FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL migrations, and container-based deployment.
questions.answerStructureHint: Walk from pull request to production: deterministic build, layered checks, artifact handling, environments, migrations, secret handling, deployment, verification, and rollback.

expectedPoints.label: Builds and verifies deterministically
expectedPoints.description: Uses pinned dependencies and clean runners, then runs formatting, lint, type checks, unit and integration tests, security scans, and production builds with fast feedback ordering.

expectedPoints.label: Promotes one immutable artifact
expectedPoints.description: Tags the source revision, creates versioned frontend and container artifacts once, signs or records provenance where needed, and injects environment configuration at deploy time.

expectedPoints.label: Controls migration and production rollout
expectedPoints.description: Runs backward-compatible migrations, obtains credentials from a secret manager, deploys gradually with health checks, performs smoke tests, observes release metrics, and supports rollback.

followUps.prompt: Which checks belong before merge, and which can run after merge without slowing developers too much?

hu.title: Delivery pipeline tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezz CI/CD pipeline-t Next.js frontendhez, Node.js vagy FastAPI backendhez, PostgreSQL migrationhöz és container-based deploymenthez.
hu.answerStructureHint: Menj végig pull requesttől productionig: deterministic build, layered check, artifact handling, environment, migration, secret handling, deployment, verification és rollback.

wcp_ci.label: Deterministic módon buildel és verify-ol
wcp_ci.description: Pinned dependencyt és clean runnert használ, majd formattingot, lintet, type checket, unit és integration testet, security scant és production buildet futtat fast-feedback sorrendben.

wcp_artifact.label: Egy immutable artifactot promotionöl
wcp_artifact.description: Tageli a source revisiont, egyszer készít versioned frontend- és container artifactot, szükség szerint provenance-et rögzít, az environment configurationt pedig deploy time injectálja.

wcp_release.label: Kontrollálja a migrationt és production rolloutot
wcp_release.description: Backward-compatible migrationt futtat, secret managerből kap credentialt, health checkkel fokozatosan deployol, smoke testet végez, release metricet figyel és rollbacket támogat.

followUps.wingos_ci_pipeline_001_followup: Mely check tartozik merge elé, és mely futhat merge után anélkül, hogy túlzottan lassítaná a developereket?

questions.title: Reviewing a production container
questions.prompt: What would you review in a Dockerfile and container setup before calling a web service production-ready?
questions.answerStructureHint: Cover reproducibility, image size and attack surface, runtime user, configuration and secrets, health and shutdown, resources, logging, and scanning.

expectedPoints.label: Creates a small reproducible image
expectedPoints.description: Pins base and dependencies, uses multi-stage builds, copies only runtime files, respects a .dockerignore, and avoids unnecessary package managers or build tools in the final image.

expectedPoints.label: Hardens runtime behaviour
expectedPoints.description: Runs as a non-root user, uses read-only or limited filesystem where possible, receives secrets at runtime, exposes health checks, and handles termination signals gracefully.

expectedPoints.label: Makes the container operable
expectedPoints.description: Writes structured logs to standard output, defines resource expectations, scans image and dependencies, records version metadata, and tests startup and shutdown.

followUps.prompt: Why should database migrations usually not run independently in every application replica at startup?

hu.title: Production container review-ja
hu.prompt: Mit review-znál egy Dockerfile-ban és container setupban, mielőtt production-readynek nevezel egy web service-t?
hu.answerStructureHint: Térj ki reproducibilityre, image size-ra és attack surface-re, runtime userre, configurationre és secretre, healthre és shutdownra, resource-ra, loggingra és scanningre.

wcr_build.label: Kicsi, reproducible image-et készít
wcr_build.description: Pineli a base-t és dependencyt, multi-stage buildet használ, csak runtime file-t másol, tiszteletben tartja a .dockerignore-t, és kerüli a felesleges package managert vagy build toolt a final image-ben.

wcr_runtime.label: Hardenseli a runtime behaviourt
wcr_runtime.description: Non-root userként fut, lehetőség szerint read-only vagy limited filesystemet használ, runtime kap secretet, health checket ad és graceful módon kezeli a termination signalt.

wcr_operate.label: Operálhatóvá teszi a containert
wcr_operate.description: Structured logot ír standard outputra, resource expectationt definiál, image-et és dependencyt scannel, version metadata-t rögzít, és startupot, valamint shutdownt tesztel.

followUps.wingos_container_review_001_followup: Miért ne fusson általában database migration minden application replica startupjakor egymástól függetlenül?

questions.title: Releasing a risky feature safely
questions.prompt: A new AI feature also needs a database schema change. Describe a release plan that lets you stop exposure or roll back without losing data.
questions.answerStructureHint: Define compatibility phases, exposure control, migration order, monitoring and rollback criteria, data backfill, and cleanup only after safety is proven.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a compatible migration sequence
expectedPoints.description: Adds nullable or backward-compatible structures first, deploys code that can work with old and new states, and performs bounded observable backfill before enforcing new constraints.

expectedPoints.label: Separates deployment from exposure
expectedPoints.description: Deploys dark or behind a feature flag, enables internal and pilot cohorts gradually, and defines health, quality, privacy, and cost thresholds for continuing.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps a realistic reversal path
expectedPoints.description: Can disable the feature without reverting data, keeps old code compatible, makes jobs idempotent, checks queued work, and postpones destructive cleanup until the rollback window closes.

followUps.prompt: What would make application rollback unsafe even if the previous container image is available?

hu.title: Kockázatos feature biztonságos release-e
hu.prompt: Egy új AI feature database schema change-et is igényel. Írj release tervet, amelyben exposure leállítható vagy rollbackelhető data loss nélkül.
hu.answerStructureHint: Definiálj compatibility phase-t, exposure controlt, migration ordert, monitoring- és rollback criteriont, data backfillt és csak bizonyított safety utáni cleanupot.

wsr_expand.label: Compatible migration sequence-et használ
wsr_expand.description: Először nullable vagy backward-compatible struktúrát ad, old és new state-t kezelő kódot deployol, majd bounded observable backfillt végez új constraint kikényszerítése előtt.

wsr_exposure.label: Szétválasztja a deploymentet és exposure-t
wsr_exposure.description: Dark módon vagy feature flag mögött deployol, internal és pilot cohortnak fokozatosan engedélyez, és health, quality, privacy, valamint cost thresholdot definiál.

wsr_rollback.label: Reális reversal pathot tart fenn
wsr_rollback.description: Data revert nélkül ki tudja kapcsolni a feature-t, old code compatibilityt tart, idempotent jobot használ, ellenőrzi a queued worköt és a destructive cleanupot a rollback window utánra hagyja.

followUps.wingos_safe_release_001_followup: Mitől lehet unsafe az application rollback akkor is, ha az előző container image elérhető?

questions.title: Handling secrets in CI/CD
questions.prompt: How would you handle database, cloud, and LLM-provider credentials across pull requests, CI, staging, and production?
questions.answerStructureHint: Discuss secret stores, short-lived identity, environment isolation, least privilege, fork safety, redaction, rotation, auditing, and incident response.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps secrets out of source and artifacts
expectedPoints.description: Uses a managed secret store or CI secret facility, injects values only at runtime, prevents them from entering images, caches, logs, build outputs, or client bundles.

expectedPoints.label: Uses scoped short-lived access
expectedPoints.description: Prefers workload identity or short-lived tokens, separates staging and production accounts, applies least privilege, and withholds production secrets from untrusted pull requests or forks.

expectedPoints.label: Manages the secret lifecycle
expectedPoints.description: Defines ownership, rotation and revocation, audits access, scans for accidental exposure, and has a response path that replaces credentials and checks misuse.

followUps.prompt: What should happen immediately after a credential appears in a Git commit, even if the commit is deleted?

hu.title: Secret-kezelés CI/CD-ben
hu.prompt: Hogyan kezelnéd a database, cloud és LLM-provider credentialt pull request, CI, staging és production környezetben?
hu.answerStructureHint: Beszélj secret store-ról, short-lived identityről, environment isolationről, least privilege-ről, fork safetyről, redactionről, rotationről, auditingról és incident response-ról.

wcs_store.label: Source-on és artifacton kívül tartja a secretet
wcs_store.description: Managed secret store-t vagy CI secret facilityt használ, csak runtime injectál, és megakadályozza, hogy image-be, cache-be, logba, build outputba vagy client bundle-be kerüljön.

wcs_identity.label: Scoped short-lived accesst használ
wcs_identity.description: Workload identityt vagy short-lived tokent preferál, elválasztja a staging és production accountot, least privilege-et alkalmaz, és untrusted pull requesttől vagy forktól elzárja a production secretet.

wcs_lifecycle.label: Kezeli a secret lifecycle-t
wcs_lifecycle.description: Ownershipet, rotationt és revocationt definiál, access auditot és exposure scant végez, valamint credential-cserét és misuse checket tartalmazó response pathot használ.

followUps.wingos_ci_secrets_001_followup: Mi történjen azonnal, ha egy credential Git commitba kerül, még akkor is, ha a commitot törlik?

questions.title: Observing a critical user journey
questions.prompt: Design observability for the journey “teacher creates an assignment, AI generates a draft, teacher reviews it, and students receive it.”
questions.answerStructureHint: Define user-visible stages and SLIs, then connect logs, metrics, traces, correlation, model metadata, privacy, dashboards, alerts, and runbooks.

expectedPoints.label: Defines user-centred service indicators
expectedPoints.description: Measures assignment save and publish success, AI job completion and latency, review or edit behaviour, delivery delay, and failures by stable category and relevant segment.

expectedPoints.label: Correlates the distributed work
expectedPoints.description: Propagates request, job, assignment, and trace identifiers through frontend, API, queue, worker, provider call, and database while avoiding personal identifiers in metric labels.

expectedPoints.label: Turns signals into safe operations
expectedPoints.description: Uses structured privacy-aware logs, model and prompt version metadata, SLO-based alerts, dashboards, release annotations, and runbooks with ownership and escalation.

followUps.prompt: Which metrics would you deliberately not label by student or classroom id?

hu.title: Critical user journey megfigyelése
hu.prompt: Tervezz observabilityt a következő journeyhez: teacher assignmentet készít, AI draftot generál, teacher review-zza, majd a student megkapja.
hu.answerStructureHint: Definiáld a user-visible stage-et és SLI-t, majd kösd össze a logot, metricet, trace-t, correlationt, model metadata-t, privacyt, dashboardot, alertet és runbookot.

wo_journey.label: User-centred service indicatort definiál
wo_journey.description: Assignment save- és publish successt, AI job completiont és latencyt, review- vagy edit behaviourt, delivery delayt és stable category, valamint releváns segment szerinti failure-t mér.

wo_connect.label: Korrelálja a distributed worköt
wo_connect.description: Request-, job-, assignment- és trace identifiert propagál frontend, API, queue, worker, provider call és database között, personal identifier nélkül a metric labelben.

wo_operate.label: Safe operationné alakítja a jeleket
wo_operate.description: Structured privacy-aware logot, model- és prompt-version metadata-t, SLO-based alertet, dashboardot, release annotationt és ownershippel, escalationnel ellátott runbookot használ.

followUps.wingos_observability_001_followup: Mely metricet nem labelölnéd szándékosan student vagy classroom id-val?

questions.title: Diagnosing intermittent assignment failures
questions.prompt: Teachers report that publishing occasionally fails, but retrying usually works. How would you investigate from first signal to verified fix?
questions.answerStructureHint: Triage impact, use correlation and segmentation, inspect traces and dependency behaviour, distinguish safe retry from duplicate effects, mitigate, fix, and verify.

expectedPoints.label: Establishes scope and severity
expectedPoints.description: Checks affected users, languages, versions, endpoints, times, data integrity, duplicate outcomes, and whether the problem is still active.

expectedPoints.label: Uses correlated evidence to isolate the cause
expectedPoints.description: Follows request or job ids through structured logs and traces, compares successful and failed cases, and checks timeouts, pool exhaustion, locks, provider errors, and deployment changes.

expectedPoints.label: Mitigates and verifies without creating duplicates
expectedPoints.description: Applies a bounded mitigation, protects side effects with idempotency, fixes the root cause, adds a regression test and alert, and verifies journey metrics after release.

followUps.prompt: When can a retry make this incident worse?

hu.title: Intermittent assignment failure diagnosztizálása
hu.prompt: A teacherek szerint a publish néha sikertelen, de retry után általában működik. Hogyan vizsgálnád ki az első jeltől a verified fixig?
hu.answerStructureHint: Triage-old az impactot, használj correlationt és segmentationt, vizsgáld a trace-t és dependency behaviourt, válaszd szét a safe retryt a duplicate effecttől, mitigate-elj, fixelj és verify-olj.

wi_triage.label: Megállapítja a scope-ot és severityt
wi_triage.description: Ellenőrzi az érintett usereket, language-et, versiont, endpointot, időt, data integrityt, duplicate outcome-ot és hogy a probléma még aktív-e.

wi_trace.label: Korrelált evidence-dzsel izolálja az okot
wi_trace.description: Request- vagy job id alapján követ structured logot és trace-t, successful és failed case-t hasonlít, és timeoutot, pool exhaustiont, lockot, provider errort, valamint deployment change-et ellenőriz.

wi_fix.label: Duplicate létrehozása nélkül mitigate-el és verify-ol
wi_fix.description: Bounded mitigationt alkalmaz, idempotencyvel védi a side effectet, root cause-t javít, regression testet és alertet ad, majd release után journey metricet verify-ol.

followUps.wingos_incident_001_followup: Mikor ronthatja a retry ezt az incidentet?

questions.title: Using TypeScript at system boundaries
questions.prompt: How do you use TypeScript to improve a full-stack codebase without confusing compile-time guarantees with runtime safety?
questions.answerStructureHint: Discuss strictness, domain types, narrowing, unknown versus any, generated or shared contracts, runtime schemas, and avoiding unsafe assertions.

expectedPoints.label: Models meaningful states
expectedPoints.description: Uses strict mode, discriminated unions, narrow domain types, readonly where useful, and exhaustive checks to make invalid states harder to represent.

expectedPoints.label: Treats external values as unknown
expectedPoints.description: Uses unknown and narrowing or a runtime schema for network, storage, environment, and provider data instead of any or unchecked type assertions.

expectedPoints.label: Coordinates contracts without hiding runtime checks
expectedPoints.description: Uses OpenAPI or schema-derived types or carefully shared definitions to reduce drift, while the server and database remain the authoritative enforcement points.

followUps.prompt: Give an example where a type assertion can hide a production bug.

hu.title: TypeScript használata system boundary-kon
hu.prompt: Hogyan használod a TypeScriptet egy full-stack codebase javítására anélkül, hogy a compile-time guarantee-t runtime safetynek tekintenéd?
hu.answerStructureHint: Beszélj strictnessről, domain type-ról, narrowingról, unknown és any különbségéről, generated vagy shared contractról, runtime schemáról és unsafe assertion kerüléséről.

wtb_types.label: Jelentéssel bíró state-et modellez
wtb_types.description: Strict mode-ot, discriminated uniont, narrow domain type-ot, szükség szerint readonly-t és exhaustive checket használ az invalid state nehezebb reprezentálásához.

wtb_unknown.label: Unknownként kezeli a külső értéket
wtb_unknown.description: Unknown és narrowing vagy runtime schema segítségével kezeli a network, storage, environment és provider adatot any vagy unchecked type assertion helyett.

wtb_contract.label: Runtime check elrejtése nélkül koordinál contractot
wtb_contract.description: OpenAPI- vagy schema-derived type-ot, illetve gondosan shared definitiont használ drift csökkentésére, miközben a server és database marad authoritative enforcement point.

followUps.wingos_typescript_boundary_001_followup: Mondj példát, amikor egy type assertion production bugot rejt el.

questions.title: Choosing state placement in React
questions.prompt: In a complex teacher editor, how do you decide whether state belongs in local component state, a reducer, context, a global client store, the URL, or server-state tooling?
questions.answerStructureHint: Classify state by ownership, persistence and source of truth, then explain update complexity, sharing, URL semantics, server cache, forms, and derived data.

expectedPoints.label: Classifies state before choosing a tool
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes local UI state, complex local transitions, cross-tree UI state, shareable URL state, server-owned remote state, and persistent draft data.

expectedPoints.label: Matches the tool to ownership and behaviour
expectedPoints.description: Uses local state by default, reducer for coordinated transitions, context for stable shared dependencies, a store for broad client-owned state, and query tooling for remote cache.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids duplicate and derived state
expectedPoints.description: Keeps one authoritative source, derives view values during render or memoized calculation, and defines save, conflict, reset, and optimistic-update behaviour explicitly.

hu.title: State placement választása Reactben
hu.prompt: Egy komplex teacher editorban hogyan döntöd el, hogy a state local component state-be, reducerbe, contextbe, global client store-ba, URL-be vagy server-state toolingba kerüljön?
hu.answerStructureHint: Osztályozd a state-et ownership, persistence és source of truth szerint, majd magyarázd el az update complexityt, sharinget, URL semanticset, server cache-t, formot és derived datát.

wrs_classify.label: Tool választás előtt osztályozza a state-et
wrs_classify.description: Megkülönbözteti a local UI state-et, complex local transitiont, cross-tree UI state-et, shareable URL state-et, server-owned remote state-et és persistent draft datát.

wrs_tool.label: A toolt ownershiphöz és behaviourhöz illeszti
wrs_tool.description: Local state-et használ defaultként, reducert coordinated transitionhöz, contextet stable shared dependencyhez, store-t broad client-owned state-hez és query toolingot remote cache-hez.

wrs_avoid.label: Kerüli a duplicate és derived state-et
wrs_avoid.description: Egy authoritative source-t tart, view value-t render vagy memoized calculation közben derive-ol, és explicit definiálja a save, conflict, reset és optimistic-update viselkedést.

questions.title: Investigating a slow React screen
questions.prompt: A multilingual exercise editor becomes sluggish with large content and frequent autosave. How would you diagnose and improve it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Measure first, separate network, rendering, JavaScript and layout costs, find the expensive path, then apply focused rendering, state, scheduling, and data-flow changes.

expectedPoints.label: Profiles before optimizing
expectedPoints.description: Uses browser performance tools and React Profiler, reproduces with realistic data, and checks network, main-thread long tasks, commits, layout, memory, and autosave timing.

expectedPoints.label: Finds the actual expensive boundary
expectedPoints.description: Checks broad state updates, unstable props, expensive derived calculations, large DOM, controlled-input fan-out, blocking parsing, repeated requests, and layout thrashing.

expectedPoints.label: Applies evidence-based fixes
expectedPoints.description: Localizes state, normalizes or splits components, memoizes only proven work, virtualizes long lists, debounces or batches autosave, moves heavy work off the main thread, and remeasures.

followUps.prompt: Why can adding useMemo everywhere make the code worse without fixing performance?

hu.title: Lassú React screen kivizsgálása
hu.prompt: Egy multilingual exercise editor nagy content és gyakori autosave mellett akadozik. Hogyan diagnosztizálnád és javítanád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Először mérj, válaszd szét a network, rendering, JavaScript és layout costot, keresd meg az expensive pathot, majd célzott rendering-, state-, scheduling- és data-flow változtatást alkalmazz.

wrp_measure.label: Optimalizálás előtt profiloz
wrp_measure.description: Browser performance toolt és React Profilert használ, realistic datával reprodukál, és networköt, main-thread long taskot, commitot, layoutot, memoryt és autosave timingot ellenőriz.

wrp_cause.label: Megtalálja a valódi expensive boundary-t
wrp_cause.description: Broad state update-et, unstable propot, expensive derived calculationt, large DOM-ot, controlled-input fan-outot, blocking parsingot, repeated requestet és layout thrashinget ellenőriz.

wrp_fix.label: Evidence-based fixet alkalmaz
wrp_fix.description: Lokalizálja a state-et, normalizál vagy bont componentet, csak proven worköt memoizál, hosszú listát virtualizál, autosave-et debounce-ol vagy batch-el, heavy worköt kivesz main threadről, majd újramér.

followUps.wingos_react_performance_001_followup: Miért ronthatja a kódot a mindenhová tett useMemo performance-javulás nélkül?

questions.title: Testing a full-stack AI-assisted feature
questions.prompt: Propose a practical test strategy for an AI-assisted assignment feature from UI to database and model provider.
questions.answerStructureHint: Separate deterministic code from probabilistic behaviour, then cover unit, contract, integration, component, end-to-end, evaluation, security, accessibility, and production checks.

expectedPoints.label: Uses tests at the right boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Unit-tests pure rules, contract-tests API shapes and provider adapters, integration-tests the real database and queue, component-tests UI behaviour, and keeps a few critical end-to-end journeys.

expectedPoints.label: Tests probabilistic behaviour appropriately
expectedPoints.description: Mocks provider mechanics in deterministic suites, evaluates real models with versioned representative cases and rubrics, and avoids exact-string assertions for open-ended output.

expectedPoints.label: Covers cross-cutting product risks
expectedPoints.description: Includes authorization isolation, prompt injection, privacy and logging, accessibility, multilingual cases, migrations, retries and idempotency, performance, release smoke tests, and production monitoring.

hu.title: Full-stack AI-assisted feature tesztelése
hu.prompt: Javasolj gyakorlati test strategyt egy AI-assisted assignment feature-höz UI-tól database-en és model provideren át.
hu.answerStructureHint: Válaszd szét a deterministic code-ot a probabilistic behaviourtől, majd térj ki unit, contract, integration, component, end-to-end, evaluation, security, accessibility és production checkre.

wts_layers.label: A megfelelő boundary-kon használ tesztet
wts_layers.description: Unit testeli a pure rule-t, contract testeli az API shape-et és provider adaptert, integration testeli a valódi database-t és queue-t, component testeli a UI behaviourt, és néhány critical end-to-end journeyt tart.

wts_ai.label: Megfelelően teszteli a probabilistic behaviourt
wts_ai.description: Deterministic suite-ben mockolja a provider mechanicset, valódi modellt versioned representative case-szel és rubrickal értékel, és kerüli az exact-string assertiont open-ended outputnál.

wts_quality.label: Lefedi a cross-cutting product risket
wts_quality.description: Authorization isolationt, prompt injectiont, privacyt és loggingot, accessibilityt, multilingual case-t, migrationt, retryt és idempotencyt, performance-t, release smoke testet és production monitoringot tartalmaz.
